summary: Tested DJ Lab end-to-end. Backend: 8/8 pytest passed (/api/, /api/tracks, /api/tracks/url for demo + invalid key, /api/mixes POST+GET persistence, validation). Frontend: UI renders correctly at 1920x800 with all decks, mixer, EQ, faders, record, library, desktop-only overlay behavior. Track library loads 6 demo tracks and Load-to-Deck-A correctly updates deck title. CRITICAL finding: SoundHelix demo tracks are blocked by CORS when loaded via Web Audio API (crossOrigin='anonymous') — demo audio never actually plays, breaking Play button state, waveform render, sync, and recording.

minor.issue: Returns 503 (S3 not configured) for any unknown key even though the error is really 'key not found'. Consider returning 404 when key is missing in demo list AND S3 is not configured (currently only 503). Acceptable per spec.

ui_bugs.issue: Play button does not change icon/state after click because underlying <audio>.play() throws NotSupportedError. Root cause is upstream CORS failure on SoundHelix.com (see integration_issues). Play button logic itself is fine; state flip is gated on successful play().
Missing data-testid attribute (e.g. data-testid='desktop-only-overlay'). Functionality works (lg:hidden on overlay, hidden lg:flex on dj-lab-root), but cannot be programmatically targeted in tests.

integration_issues.flow: Demo track playback (ALL decks, waveform, sync, record)
integration_issues.issue: SoundHelix.com does NOT return Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. The Deck mounts an HTMLAudioElement with crossOrigin='anonymous' (required for MediaElementSource + Wavesurfer analyser). Browser blocks the fetch with 'No Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is present', audio element reports 'The element has no supported sources', and play() rejects. Net effect: NONE of the 6 demo tracks are playable in the browser. This defeats the 'demo mode SoundHelix fallback' requirement.

issues: Design is clean, dark theme, proper contrast on DJ LAB header, deck titles, crossfader. No contrast issues spotted at 1920x800.

action_items: CRITICAL — Fix demo track CORS: SoundHelix does not send CORS headers, so demo audio cannot play through Web Audio API. Recommended fix: add a backend proxy endpoint (e.g. GET /api/tracks/stream?key=demo-1) that fetches the remote mp3 on the server and streams it back with proper CORS + Accept-Ranges, then have /api/tracks/url (for source='demo') return that proxy URL instead of the raw soundhelix.com URL. Alternative: bundle 2-3 CC0 mp3s in /app/frontend/public/demo-audio and reference them as same-origin URLs.

MEDIUM — After CORS is fixed, revalidate full Play → Wavesurfer ready → Sync → Record flow and ensure mixdown .webm has non-zero size (currently records 0.00 MB because master bus has no actual audio input).

LOW — Add data-testid='desktop-only-overlay' to the root div of /app/frontend/src/components/dj/DesktopOnlyOverlay.jsx so responsive behavior can be asserted directly instead of via computed style on dj-lab-root.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 185: logger.warning(...) is called inside list_tracks() but logger is defined on line 245 — AFTER app + router setup. Works only because the warning is inside an async function evaluated at call time; still a code smell. Move logging.basicConfig + logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) to the top of the file (right after imports).

server.py /api/mixes storage mixes datetime as ISO string but response_model=SavedMix expects datetime. Conversion happens on read (line 226-227) but not on write — acceptable but inconsistent. Consider using datetime objects natively with motor.
server.py: S3 prefix gate (line 200-202) is effectively dead code — the 'pass' branch never enforces the prefix. Either remove or implement a 403 if key does not match S3_PREFIX.

Deck.jsx play() silently swallows errors with console.error. The UI does not surface playback failures to the user (no toast / error badge). Users will click Play repeatedly with no feedback when demo CORS fails. Add an on-screen error state in the deck.

DJLab.jsx line 16: fetch uses template-literal with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL but no null guard — if the env var is missing, fetch('undefined/api/') will hit the current origin and fail confusingly. Add a guard or throw at startup.
TrackLibrary.jsx line 11: same REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL usage without guard.

success_rate.frontend: ~70% (UI renders + wired; actual audio playback blocked by CORS)

seed_data_creation: Created one TEST_mix_pytest record in MongoDB via /api/mixes POST. Does not interfere with app (no list view in MVP).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid (all 8 API tests pass). The core remaining problem is a third-party CORS issue preventing demo audio playback — UI-level tests that do NOT depend on actual audio (load track into deck, title change, knob/fader presence, crossfader value, record toggle visual state, library open/close, desktop-overlay responsive) all pass. Any test that needs audio to actually play (Play icon → Pause icon, waveform render, sync engage, non-empty mix recording) will fail until main agent fixes the demo audio source (proxy or local files). Backend test: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_djlab_api.py.
rca of the issue: RCA for demo playback failure: (1) Deck.jsx mounts <audio crossOrigin='anonymous'> because MediaElementSource + Wavesurfer analyser require CORS-enabled audio. (2) /api/tracks/url returns the raw SoundHelix URL for source='demo' (server.py line 198). (3) SoundHelix.com does NOT include Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. (4) Browser CORS preflight/fetch fails → audio element state = no supported sources → audio.play() rejects with NotSupportedError → setDeck({playing:true}) never runs → Play button icon stays on ▶. Reproduction: open app at 1920x800, click Track Library, click Deck A on any demo row, click Play on Deck A, open DevTools Network — see ERR_FAILED on soundhelix.com mp3 with CORS error in console. Mitigation: add /api/tracks/stream proxy in FastAPI that pipes the upstream mp3 through with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and Accept-Ranges: bytes, OR bundle a couple of CC0 mp3s under /app/frontend/public and list them as same-origin URLs.
